entry.title: Re: Unable to Rename Apps in SAP Build Apps - Free Edition
entry.content:
What you can sort of do is download your project, then create a new project via import. The project in your "lobby" will now have the new name:

But strangely, the name after opening i will have the same name:

entry.title: Re: Help scroll view position app WEB
entry.content: ARe you saying you want to scroll the page so the header disappears but the search bar remains? So the complexity here is that instead of wanting the 1st section to remain, which would be easy to keep, you want the top section to disappear on scroll but then the middle section to remain and the bottom section to keep scrolling?

entry.title: Re: Bar chart in SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
Hey @LudoNoens ... how are you?

@YashminBehera I tested quickly with Norhwind products and was able to easily paginate and have the chart update and work on each page.

entry.title: 🇨🇭 SAP Build: Create Event-Based Processes (Risch-Rotkreuz, Switzerland)
entry.content:
This is the all-new SAP CodeJam for SAP Build low-code/no-code tooling that focuses on giving you hands-on experience for building event-triggered processes with SAP Build Process Automation.

You will build an event-based scenario that lets users create S/4HANA business partners, setting off a process where:

  1. An employee approves the creation of a site access badge.
  2. A second employee, determined based on the details of the business partner, may also need to approve the badge.
  3. The process calls an external, 3rd-party badge system and waits to be called back.
  4. A user creates the badge and the badge system calls back the process instance.
  5. A notification with the badge ID is sent.

The scenario is built in 2 halves:

  • SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh: You will get to know all about events, CloudEvents, events in the SAP landscape, the process for publishing and subscribing to events, and your way around SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh. Then you will create a REST Delivery Point in advanced event mesh to forward events to your SAP Build on your SAP BTP trial account.
  • SAP Build Process Automation: You will create a process from scratch to handle the issuance of site access badges. You will learn the basics of SAP Build Process Automation (projects, triggers, conditions, creating instances, monitoring, and the Inbox), and also use some of the more recent or advanced features (event triggers, form events, waiting for a API callback).

Prerequisites

  • Bring your own laptop with power adapter and have a supported Web browser such as Google Chrome or Firefox installed on your laptop.
  • It can be helpful to bring a tablet or second laptop, if you have.
  • Complete these prerequisites BEFORE you come: 
    • Introduction to the CodeJam
    • Prerequisites

Location and Date 

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Wednesday, May 21, 2025 from 09:00 - 17:00 local time in Rotkreuz, Switzerland (CET). The language of the event and the content will be English.

Event Address: GAMBIT Consulting AG, Suurstoffi 22, 6343 Risch-Rotkreuz, Switzerland (8th floor)

  • The event is kindly being hosted by GAMBIT Consulting – THANK YOU!
  • The instructors are SAP Developer Advocate @ajmaradiaga and @Dan_Wroblewski.

Please note that spaces are limited! We can only accommodate 20 attendees. If you want to attend, register by clicking the Yes button in the Will you be attending? section above to the right. About 2 weeks before the event, you will be asked to confirm your attendance.

entry.title: Re: My first steps with BTP destinations (S/4HANA) in AppGyver
entry.content:
@kishore91 I tested both AppgyverEnabled and Appgyver.Enabled and they still seem to work, as I would have expected so that Build Apps is backward compatible. That said, I would use the current BuildApps.Enabled

And remember the BuildApps.ApiType, which is important.

entry.title: 🇮🇹 SAP Build: Create Apps, Processes and Sites Without Code (Brescia, Italy)
entry.content:
REGISTRATION CLOSED: This CodeJam is full. However, you can still register and you will be put on a waiting list. If a space opens up  we will let you know.

This is the all-new and revised SAP CodeJam event for SAP Build low-code/no-code tooling and will give you a hands-on experience with SAP Build for building apps, business processes, and business sites without coding. 

You will get to know the environment by creating:

  • A business app for an online store (SAP Build Apps).
  • A business process for approving purchases (SAP Build Process Automation).
  • An apps landing page (SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition)
  • We will also demo desktop automations and a full business site (SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition).

Prerequisites

  • Bring your own laptop with power adapter and have a supported Web browser such as Google Chrome or Firefox installed on your laptop.
  • It can be helpful to bring a tablet or second laptop, if you have.
  • Complete these prerequisites BEFORE you come: Set Up Prerequisites for SAP Build CodeJam 

Location and Date 

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Monday, May 19, 2025 from 09:00 - 17:00 local time in Brescia, Italy (CET). The language of the event and the content will be English.

Event Address: Via Antonio Panigada, 15, 25126 Brescia BS, Italy 

  • The event is kindly being hosted by Regesta SPA – THANK YOU!
  • The instructor is SAP Developer Advocate @Dan_Wroblewski.

Please note that spaces are limited! We can only accommodate 30 attendees. If you want to attend, register by clicking the Yes button in the Will you be attending? section above to the right. A few weeks before the event, we will ask you to confirm your attendance.

entry.title: 🇫🇷 SAP Build: Create Event-Based Processes (Paris, France)
entry.content:
This is the all-new SAP CodeJam for SAP Build low-code/no-code tooling that focuses on giving you hands-on experience for building event-triggered processes with SAP Build Process Automation.

You will build an event-based scenario that lets users create S/4HANA business partners, setting off a process where:

  1. An employee approves the creation of a site access badge.
  2. A second employee, determined based on the details of the business partner, may also need to approve the badge.
  3. The process calls an external, 3rd-party badge system and waits to be called back.
  4. A user creates the badge and the badge system calls back the process instance.
  5. A notification with the badge ID is sent.

The scenario is built in 2 halves:

  • SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh: You will get to know all about events, CloudEvents, events in the SAP landscape, the process for publishing and subscribing to events, and your way around SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh. Then you will create a REST Delivery Point in advanced event mesh to forward events to your SAP Build on your SAP BTP trial account.
  • SAP Build Process Automation: You will create a process from scratch to handle the issuance of site access badges. You will learn the basics of SAP Build Process Automation (projects, triggers, conditions, creating instances, monitoring, and the Inbox), and also use some of the more recent or advanced features (event triggers, form events, waiting for a API callback).

Prerequisites

  • Bring your own laptop with power adapter and have a supported Web browser such as Google Chrome or Firefox installed on your laptop.
  • It can be helpful to bring a tablet or second laptop, if you have.
  • Complete these prerequisites BEFORE you come: 
    • Introduction to the CodeJam
    • Prerequisites

Location and Date 

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Tuesday, June 10, 2025 from 09:30 - 16:00 local time in Paris, France (CET). The language of the event and the content will be English.

Event Address: Nagarro, Tour Franklin, 100-101 Terr. Boieldieu, 92800 Puteaux

  • The event is kindly being hosted by Nagarro– THANK YOU!
  • The instructors are SAP Developer Advocate @ajmaradiaga and @Dan_Wroblewski.

Please note that spaces are limited! We can only accommodate 30 attendees. If you want to attend, register by clicking the Yes button in the Will you be attending? section above to the right. About 2 weeks before the event, you will be asked to confirm your attendance.

entry.title: Re: New "Wait for API Call" Integrates Processes with External Systems
entry.content:
1. Yes, the external system would have to have an API that enables sending additional fields, and for that matter, would have to know how to call the process automation API (with API key and GUID and payload).

2. Yes, executionId in the process instance ID.

entry.title: Re: 🇫🇷 SAP Build: Create Event-Based Processes (Paris, France)
entry.content: It was changed to June 10 so not to fall on the holiday. Do you still see anywhere where it says the old date?

entry.title: Re: How to Approve/Reject Task via API in SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
@padma_kumar_perumal Can you provide more information, for example:

  • Are you using Public or other environment?
  • How are you making the call?
  • Are you using a destination? If so, how is it configured?
  • Are your roles given to your default user or custom user?
  • How did you trigger the process? And whose credentials were used?

On my trial account, I created a new process with API trigger, deployed to a custom environment, then through Postman I was able to retrieve the context and logs using just the API key info for the service.

I am assuming you are doing something different since using just the ID/secret to connect would not involve any "user".

entry.title: Re: Deep entity search in Get Record Collection in SAP BU...
entry.content: I'm not an expert here, but from a little research it seems that OData V2 does not allow filtering based on nested property which is 1-to-many. I'm assuming that is your case.

entry.title: SAP Community Escape House for April – SAP Build 👷‍♀️🏠
entry.content:
The 👉 SAP Community Escape House has a new addition!!

We've built a garage for our workshop to build all kinds of stuff using SAP Build. Use your wits and knowledge about SAP Build – specifically SAP Build Process Automation – to try to escape.

WARNING: It will be really tough to escape – you have been warned! So I will be providing tips throughout the month to help you through, so subscribe to this post.

For this year, all SAP Developer Challenges created by the Developer Advocates will happen in the SAP Community Escape House (shout out to @nicoschoenteich for his amazing creativity). Each month we will unlock a new room in the house with a specific focus. Completing a challenge means successfully escaping the room by solving a series of coding – or no-code 😉 – tasks/questions. This month is all about one of the tools inside SAP Build.

Here are our previous challenges:

  • January: UI5
  • February: Integration Suite
  • March: AI

Visit the SAP Community Escape House and enter your numeric SAP Community user ID.

If you don't know your user ID, click Need Help? This will display a link to your community settings page.

Then you will see your ID here:

Once you see the SAP Community Escape House, select the SAP Build garage.

The user interface of an escape room includes the room itself as well as a terminal. You can click the objects in the room to receive tips.

Like in a real (physical) escape room, you will have to try out a few things and explore the room until you receive helpful tips and, eventually, instructions. These tips and instructions are printed in the terminal next to the room.

The terminal is also used to submit solutions to tasks. Read the instructions you receive carefully to understand how.

If you successfully escape the room (you will know when that happens), you will receive a badge in the SAP Community – and the unending esteem of your fellow SAP developers, business users and implementers. We will assign the badges once the challenge closes.

Good luck!

😺

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Escape House for April – SAP Build 👷‍♀️🏠
entry.content:
@Sabarim_07 If there's something specific you are having trouble with, let me know. The Mass Maintenance of Service Orders would require a destination to a DMS service and a destination to an S/4HANA system using a user with permission to the Service Orders OData service.

I have been able to get it work -- it is actually pretty cool, using processes, automations, DMS, S/4HANA, Web Call and script activities and more. I will write a blog or do a video on this sometime.

Let me know if I can help you in any way.

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Escape House for April – SAP Build 👷‍♀️🏠
entry.content:
@vishalakshmi You do not have to install the agent for this challenge.

Are you saying that when you click "Go to RBSC Portal" you get the 500 error? What landscape are you on? I'm not really an expert on RBSC, which is not specific to SAP Build Process Automation. It is used to deliver software and check licenses and export controls and perhaps other details. It took me a while to figure out what I needed to do, which I think was more complicated because I am at SAP. For you, you should just need an S-user and a technical user (which I think you create after having an S-user).

https://help.sap.com/docs/RBSC/0a64be17478d4f5ba45d14ab62b0d74c/175673b12feb41739df4f041db52fe76.html?locale=en-US

More info on RBSC in relation to SAP Build Process Automation: https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/install-desktop-agent-3-and-manage-updates

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Escape House for April – SAP Build 👷‍♀️🏠
entry.content: @jexpo I think i was too easy on you guys 💪

entry.title: Re: 🇫🇷 SAP Build: Create Event-Based Processes (Paris, France)
entry.content: Updated to Tuesday ... thanks for pointing out

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Escape House for April – SAP Build 👷‍♀️🏠
entry.content: @vishalakshmi Glad you escaped, but also glad it was a challenge 😺

entry.title: Re: How to Call OData Functions from SAP Build Apps
entry.content: @Lamiafarhat Sorry for the delayed response because of Passover holiday. Can you explain some more what is your situation?

entry.title: Now Define Variables from Pre-existing Schemas
entry.content:
SAP Build Apps now has the ability to create page variables based on existing schemas, like from other variables, data entities, and most interestingly, processes. This is really helpful.

I especially like that for every process type of schema, you can get both the input and output schemas -- more on this below.

HISTORICAL CONTEXT: I'm sure this feature was an idea for a while, but I first heard about when the new Processes flow functions were released a year ago. It was great that you could now trigger processes from SAP Build Apps without all the manual calling of an API, but you still had to create the variables for the inputs and outputs -- if you made one data type mistake the process would fail. So the answer was for the schemas to be easily inherited from the process, and now that is possible.

The Basics

When creating a new page variable, you can still define it from scratch, but now you can simply copy the schema from somewhere else:

Important to note that these definitions copy the schema onto the new variable. You can then modify the variable definition -- there is "linking".

  • Variables: There are many times I need multiple copies of the same data -- I want to modify the data but keep the original in a different variable.
  • Actions and Process: With SAP Build Apps now able to easily call actions and processes, it is helpful to not have to create your own variables to hold the returned data, something you had to do up until now.
  • Entities: This you could do with Data Variables, but there were times when you wanted to modify that data without disturbing the original data.

Use with Processes

I got to understand the feature because I recently updated tutorials for integrating SAP Build Apps with SAP Build Process Automation, and the ability to quickly create variables made the tutorial easier to understand and quicker to perform.

https://developers.sap.com/mission.build-workshop-sales-order.html

Triggering a Process

When triggering a process, all I had to do was create a page variable from the process schema, indicating:

  1. Base it on a process
  2. Use my process
  3. Use the schemas involved with the APIs for triggering a process
  4. Use the inputs of the process

One of the pains before was that essentially in SAP Build Apps, when defining the REST APIs for calling SAP Build Process Automations, you could only have one schema definition, either the input or the output. Now, I can create page variables for the inputs and for the outputs.

Here, I set up the page variable for the inputs of our process inputs, which is quite complicated.

To trigger the process, all I had to do was bind the page variable to the fields. Then, when I click the button to trigger, I drag a Trigger Process flow function, and set the Input Parameters to the context property of the page variable. No more setting object properties and each field separately.

I can still change the binding type to Object Properties and set the fields manually, or I can use a Formula to make modifications to the page variable. 

Retrieving Instances

Getting info about the process instances was even simpler, and the ability to create both input and output schemas helped.

This time I created a variable based on the Retrieve Process Instances schemas, and used the output schema.

This provided the standard fields that are part of every such API call.

Now all I had to do was bind the page variable to the UI. And in the logic, I just put the output of the Retrieve Process Instances flow function into the variable. Really easy.

It took really no time to be able to view the list of process instances.

Tutorials

Again, you can see the whole SAP Build Apps integration with SAP Build Process Automation in the revised sales order tutorials, which are now even faster to do, less than an hour to do both the process and the app. 

https://developers.sap.com/mission.build-workshop-sales-order.html

entry.title: Format and Display JSON in Your App
entry.content:
I wouldn't have thought this would be a big deal, but I recently got a call from a colleague in Ireland who was building an app for an important customer. And the customer had a requirement to retrieve data and display it in formatted JSON.

So my colleague asked: How do you you display formatted JSON?

At first, we both thought you could simply use the ENCODE_JSON formula function and then display in a Text field, but that did not work. But then I discovered (with the help of a friend 😽) that that function has a parameter, and that is all you need.

How to Do It

To demonstrate, I connected to the Northwind Product entity.

I then created a data variable that returned the first product, again, just to have some data.

On my UI, I created a container with a text box, whose content was set to a page variable, textToDisplay (with an initial value). I used the built-in style Primary Card to make it look nice.

SIDE NOTE: I used the custom width/height setting to make the container a minimum size but let it expand with the data. 

I then added a Set Page Variable in the data variable logic to set the page variable.

And I set the page variable to the formula below. Note some interesting things:

  • If you set the Spaces parameter of ENCODE_JSON, you will get the JSON nicely formatted.
  • Again, notice the formula editor has really good documentation on every function (if I would only read it 🤦).
ENCODE_JSON(data.Products1,4)

The result?

Without the Spaces parameter, it would have displayed like this:

Making It Editable

You could also put the text in an input box so a user could edit it.

Then you could provide a button to validate the JSON they type by using a simple JavaScript flow function.

I set the input to the page variable, changed the output to true/false, and used the following code:

If I click Validate with the default JSON:

If I make an error:

👉 Let me know if this was helpful, and maybe how you would improve on this.
